definition:
τελέω, -ῶ
 (τέλος), [in LXX for כָּלָה, pi., etc. ;] 
  1. to bring to an end, complete, finish: τ. δρόμον, 2Ti.4:7; τ. λόγους, Mat.7:28 19:1 26:1; τ. παραβολάς, Mat.13:53; τ. πόλεις, Mat.10:23; pass., Rev.15:8 20:3, 5 20:7; with ptcp., Mat.11:1. 
   2. to execute, perform, complete, fulfil: Luk.2:39, Act.13:29, Rom.2:27, 2Co.12:9, Gal.5:16, Jas.2:8, Rev.11:7; pass., Luk.12:50 18:31 22:37, Jhn.19:28, 30, Rev.10:7 15:1 17:17. 
   3. to pay (frequently in cl.): Mat.17:24, Rom.13:6 
 (cf. ἀπο-, δια-, ἐκ-, ἐπι-, συν-τελέω).†
 (AS)
